ogTitle: New Harbourline vs New Mapoon: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in New Harbourline and New Mapoon.
heading: New Harbourline vs New Mapoon — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between New Harbourline, Queensland and New Mapoon, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: New Mapoon has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to New Harbourline (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is New Harbourline or New Mapoon safer from flooding?
faq.answer: New Harbourline has a flood risk score of 59/100, while New Mapoon scores 39/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: New Harbourline scores 48/100 for bushfire risk. New Mapoon scores 59/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in New Harbourline or New Mapoon?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. New Mapoon has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.95% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
